attacking_style: Commits to long-range breakaway efforts on progressive or altitude stages, willing to animate races from distance rather than waiting for final climbs. Shows endurance over explosive finishing power.
team_tactics: Often given freedom to join breakaways without heavy team support; capable of operating independently without leadout or positioning assistance, suggesting autonomous role within Lotto setup.
terrain_preference: Progressive altitude stages and medium-mountain terrain where sustained effort matters more than explosive climbing. Can handle sprint finishes when breakaways fail, showing versatility beyond pure climber profile.
vulnerability: Exposed when WorldTour GC contenders launch decisive attacks in Grand Tour finales; lacks top-end acceleration to follow when race explodes at highest level, as shown when caught by Pogačar.
key_patterns: Joins animated breakaways on mountain stages, sustaining long-distance efforts (2 observations)
Catches caught before finale but survives to contest reduced sprint finishes (2 observations)
Operates without structured team support, functioning as independent attacker (2 observations)
Shows endurance on progressive climbs but lacks explosive acceleration against top-tier attacks (2 observations)
form_trajectory: positive: demonstrated progression from breakaway rider to multi-dimensional threat capable of both sustained efforts and sprint finishes in hilly terrain
